verses.text: Le Seigneur est un refuge pour quiconque vient à lui pour chercher protection. Le psalmiste fait confiance et se soumet au Seigneur. Voir aussi **71.1-3** .\n\n
Le psalmiste interpelle urgemment le Seigneur pour qu'il prête attention à sa situation désespérée ( **71.2** ; **88.3** ; **102.3** ).

31:3 For thou art my rock and my fortress,.... What he prayed for he knew him to be, and to have been in times past, and could claim his interest in him; and therefore entreats that he would appear to be to him what he was in himself, and what he had been to him; therefore for thy name's sake lead me, and guide me; either as a shepherd does his flock, gently, as they are able to bear it; into the green pastures of the word and ordinances, and beside the still waters of divine love, and to the overflowing fountain, and fulness of grace in himself;

31:4 Pull me out of the net that they have laid privily for me,.... The Ziphites, and Saul, and his men; the former intending treacherously to betray him, and the latter encompassing him about in order to take him; and such was his danger and difficulty, that he saw none but God could deliver him; and he it is that breaks the nets of men, and the snares of the devil, which they secretly lay for the people of God, that they may stumble, and fall, and be taken, and delivers them out of them;
Je remets mon esprit entre tes mains : Qu'il vive ou qu'il meure, le psalmiste fait confiance à son Seigneur. Jésus a prononcé ces mots sur la croix juste avant de mourir ( **Lc 23.46** ).
La confiance dans le Seigneur requiert la certitude qu’il a nos intérêts à cœur.
La confiance dans le Seigneur requiert la certitude qu’il a nos intérêts à cœur.
Le large désigne un endroit vaste, par opposition à un endroit « étroit » ou restreint, où peut émerger la détresse (voir **18.20** ).
Dans cette prière de miséricorde, le psalmiste insère une description de l'intensité de la souffrance et de la douleur du rejet entre deux affirmations de sa confiance ( **31.7** , **15** ).\n\n
Se consume dans la douleur : C'est une description puissante de la façon dont le désespoir affecte notre bien-être physique.

31:11 I was a reproach among all mine enemies,.... This is a common case of the people of God; and though it may be the least of their afflictions, yet it is not grateful to the flesh; and it is as it is made: under divine supports saints rejoice, and take pleasure in reproaches, that they are counted worthy to bear them, and esteem them as great riches; at other times they seize and feed upon their spirits, and are ready to break their hearts;

31:12 I am forgotten as a dead man out of mind,.... Either by his friends, being out of sight, out of mind; as even the nearest relations and acquaintance are, in process of time, when dead, Ecc 9:5; or by the Lord; which shows the weakness of his faith, the uncomfortable frame he was in, through darkness and desertion; see Ps 88:5; I am like a broken vessel; or a "perishing vessel" (c); or "a vessel of perdition" (d): the Septuagint version renders it "a lost vessel" (e);

31:13 For I have heard the slander of many,.... Both enemies and neighbours; fear was on every side; his enemies were a "Magormissabib" to him, Jer 20:3; encompassed him around, so that he was in fear from every quarter; while they took counsel together against me; how to apprehend him, and what to do with him; they devised to take away my life; nothing short of that would satisfy; but life is in the hand of God; men may devise, but God disappoints, and his counsel stands;
On ne confie pas son espoir à des êtres humains fragiles et instables, mais seulement au Seigneur. Le souffrant s'en remet à Dieu en attendant sa justice.\n\n
Mes destinées sont dans ta main : Renoncer au contrôle du moment choisi est une façon d'exprimer la soumission.
31:16 Make . . . shine—(Compare Num 6:25; Ps 4:6). Deprecating from himself, he imprecates on the wicked God's displeasure, and prays that their virulent persecution of him may be stopped.
Le psalmiste, qui ne méritait pas le déshonneur , appelle à une forme de rétribution (voir la note d'étude sur 1.6 ; voir aussi la note thématique « Prières pour la vengeance »).

31:18 Let the lying lips be put to silence,.... Being convicted of the lies told by them, and so silenced and confounded; or being cut off and destroyed, as all such will be in the Lord's own time, Ps 12:3. It is very likely the psalmist may have respect either to Doeg the Edomite, who loved lying rather than righteousness; or to others that were about Saul, who lying said to him that David sought his harm, even to take away his kingdom and his life, Ps 52:3;
Puisque les pieux sont sous la protection du Seigneur, le mal n'a aucun pouvoir sur eux.
Puisque les pieux sont sous la protection du Seigneur, le mal n'a aucun pouvoir sur eux.
Béni soit l'Éternel : Le psalmiste exprime sa joie face à son expérience de la bonté et de l'amour de Dieu. Tout au long de son épreuve, le Seigneur a pris soin de lui.
Béni soit l'Éternel : Le psalmiste exprime sa joie face à son expérience de la bonté et de l'amour de Dieu. Tout au long de son épreuve, le Seigneur a pris soin de lui.

À partir de sa propre douleur et de la conscience renouvelée de la bonté du Seigneur, le psalmiste se tourne vers ceux qui souffrent. Il offre la promesse de l'amour bienveillant de Dieu à ceux qui persévèrent dans leur foi envers le Seigneur.

À partir de sa propre douleur et de la conscience renouvelée de la bonté du Seigneur, le psalmiste se tourne vers ceux qui souffrent. Il offre la promesse de l'amour bienveillant de Dieu à ceux qui persévèrent dans leur foi envers le Seigneur.
